t_ch: 实不相瞒，公司给仙舟自傲的技术开出了合适的价码，但是实际上具体的洽谈不太顺利……
t_en: Well, to be honest, we offered a handsome price for some of Xianzhou's proud technological achievements, but negotiations didn't actually go so well...

t_ch: 不过，我相信仙舟不是一个保守的地方，「功夫不负有心人」，事情总会有突破的。
t_en: But I trust that Xianzhou isn't a conservative place. Hard work always pays off — and breakthroughs are always possible.

t_ch: 我就只能说到这种程度了，希望你见谅，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: That's about as much as I can say on this. Hope you forgive me! Hahaha.

t_ch: 有没有给化外民的建议…
t_en: Have you got any advice for an Outworlder?

t_ch: 我刚到仙舟的时候，收到了一本小册子叫《化外民也能学会的地道仙舟俗语<unbreak>100</unbreak>句》，虽然标题取得不怎么样，内容还是不错的。
t_en: I was gifted a manual when I first got here, called 100 Common Xianzhou Proverbs for Outworlders. It's a lousy title, I know, but the book itself is great.

t_ch: 书里面提到了很多仙舟人平时会用的谚语、俗语，非常实用，你要是对仙舟文化感兴趣，可以把它当做一个切入口。
t_en: The book is really practical, mentioning tons of proverbs and sayings that the Xianzhou natives commonly use. It's a fantastic window into Xianzhou culture if that's something you're interested in.

t_ch: 有缘再会。
t_en: Until we meet again.

t_ch: 有缘再会。
t_en: Until we meet again.

t_ch: 有缘再会。
t_en: Until we meet again.

t_ch: 有缘再会。
t_en: Until we meet again.

t_ch: 「息土不耗减，掘之益多」…仙舟真的有这等好东西？
t_en: "Soil that does not deplete, but self-replicates." ...is there really such incredible tech aboard Xianzhou?

t_ch: 哟，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}来了，想跟我聊聊，还是有什么别的事情？
t_en: Ah, our {F#girl}{M#boy} is back. Have you come to chat with me, or is there something else?

t_ch: 不知道你有没有听说过，叫作「息壤」。按照古籍的说法，这是指神话里自己生长、膨胀的土壤。
t_en: Have you ever heard of something called "Fallow Earth"? The ancient texts claim that it's a kind of legendary soil that grows and expands by itself.

t_ch: 「息土不耗减，掘之益多」…这样土壤不仅挖不尽，不断挖还有更多。
t_en: "Soil that does not deplete, but self-replicates"... so you never run out of soil, but instead always end up with more.

t_ch: 你相信有这种东西存在吗？巨大的建木就矗立在仙舟之上，仿佛一切神话故事都能在这里找到存在的证据。
t_en: Do you believe there's really such a thing? The giant Ambrosial Arbor grows aboard Xianzhou, as if all myths and legends can find evidence of their existence here.

t_ch: 仙舟的「息壤」也必将造福于银河…前提是，仙舟人愿意的话。
t_en: Xianzhou's Fallow Earth can benefit the entire cosmos... that is, so long as the Xianzhou natives are willing to share it.

t_ch: 想打听一点儿仙舟趣闻。
t_en: I want to ask you about some Xianzhou trivia.

t_ch: 不知道你有没有听说过，仙舟有一种材料名为「息壤」。按照古籍的说法，这是指神话里自己生长、膨胀的土壤。
t_en: I don't know if you've heard this before, but have you ever heard of something called "Fallow Earth"? According to ancient texts, it's a kind of legendary soil that grows and expands by itself.

t_ch: 「息土不耗减，掘之益多」…这样土壤不仅挖不尽，不断挖还有更多。
t_en: "Soil that does not deplete, but self-replicates"... so you never run out of soil, but instead always end up with more.

t_ch: 你相信有这种东西存在吗？我知道天才俱乐部中曾有人构想过类似的东西，「以利亚萨拉斯探针」。那是一种会利用环境，自我增殖、自我修复的机器。
t_en: You think such a thing exists? I know someone in the Genius Society once conceptualized something similar with the Elias Salas Probe. It was a machine that could self-propagate and self-repair by utilizing the environment.

t_ch: 但所有现存的类似设计全都在一定的复制周期后停转，或者说「死亡」了。也许息壤也是类似的东西吧？
t_en: But all similarly existing designs have been halted, or rather, "died" after a certain number of replication cycles. Perhaps this Fallow Earth is the same?

t_ch: 要是能证明它比公司所用的纳米机器更有效…仙舟的「息壤」也必将造福于银河。前提是，仙舟人愿意。
t_en: If we can somehow prove that it's more effective than the IPC's nanomachines... Xianzhou's Fallow Earth could certainly benefit the cosmos if the Xianzhou people agree to it.

t_ch: 建木到底是什么材料做的？
t_en: What exactly is the Ambrosial Arbor made of?

t_ch: 我也好奇，不对，打住打住！
t_en: I'm also curious about that... Wait! Stop right there!

t_ch: 在仙舟这可是顶级机密，先前那棵巨木长起来的时候你没见着吗？巡逻的云骑，还有地衡司的人…严防死守的。
t_en: It is a top secret on the Xianzhou. Didn't you see how things were when that giant tree grew? It was heavily guarded by people from the Realm-Keeping Commission and the Cloud Knights were on patrol.

t_ch: 我听闻这建木是星神造物，虽不知是真是假，但看仙舟严阵以待的架势，我劝你可别把主意打到建木上去，免得犯了他们仙舟人的忌讳。
t_en: I heard that the Ambrosial Arbor is an Aeonspawn. I'm not sure whether it is true, but seeing how seriously Xianzhou treats the issue, I suggest that you stay away from the tree and don't dabble in their taboo.

t_ch: 「要尊重当地人的风俗规矩，否则后果自负」每次出差前，公司同事都这么叮嘱我，出门在外，你也小心些吧！
t_en: Before every business trip, my colleagues always remind me to respect the local customs, or be ready to bear the consequences. You should be careful with your actions when travelling, too!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你也是神仙吗？为什么你看起来和其他神仙不一样？
t_en: {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, are you a celestial too? Why do you look different from the rest of them?

t_ch: 我应该不是吧…
t_en: I don't think I am...

t_ch: 咦？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}不是神仙吗？那你一定也是和我一样，来找神仙的吧？
t_en: Huh? You're not a celestial? Then you must be seeking celestials, just like I am?

t_ch: 其他神仙？
t_en: Other celestials?

t_ch: 对呀，这里可是九天之境，在这里生活的都是神仙！咦？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}不是神仙吗？那你一定也是和我一样，来找神仙的吧？
t_en: Yes, this is the Novenus Empyrea, where all celestials reside! Huh? {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, you're not a celestial? Then you must be seeking celestials, just like I am?

t_ch: 我也是神仙！
t_en: I'm a celestial too!

t_ch: 真的吗？恕冬生愚钝，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}并没有那种仙人气质……
t_en: Really? Forgive my dullness, but {F#big sister}{M#big brother}, you don't exude that celestial air...

t_ch: 噢噢，忘了说啦，咱叫冬生，是跟着一位神仙姐姐来这里的。不过…神仙姐姐不知道这事儿。
t_en: Ooh, I forgot to introduce myself. I'm Dongsheng. I followed a celestial lady here. But... she doesn't know that.

t_ch: 神仙姐姐是谁？
t_en: Who is this celestial lady?

t_ch: 神仙姐姐就是…就是那个特别特别神、特别特别仙的大姐姐。
t_en: She is... a lady who is both heavenly and celestial.

t_ch: 封建迷信要不得！
t_en: Let's not be superstitious!

t_ch: 村里的老人们都说，天有九重，上面有宫殿，宫殿里住着容颜不老的仙人。咱原来以为都是哄小孩子的故事，没想到这都是真的！
t_en: The older folks in the village say there are nine layers of heaven, with a palace at the very top where the eternally youthful celestials live. I thought it was just a fairy tale, but it's actually true!

t_ch: 冬生也想成为神仙，但爹说，只有修道有成的人才能去到仙宫，跟神仙一起喝桂花酒，吃大鸡腿！
t_en: I want to become a celestial too, but Dad said only those who have reached the pinnacle of cultivation may enter the Celestial Hall, drink osmanthus wine, and feast on chicken drumsticks with the gods!

t_ch: 冬生不懂修道是什么，本来以为自己这辈子都没有吃大鸡腿的福分了。结果这一晃眼，咱就来到仙宫里啦！
t_en: I don't know what cultivation is, so I thought I'd never get the chance to eat chicken drumsticks, but here I am at the Celestial Hall!

t_ch: 只是，自从到这里后，就再也没找见神仙姐姐。心里有些难过。{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，还有什么事情吗？
t_en: It's just that I haven't found the celestial lady since I arrived, and that makes me sad. {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, is there anything else you want to ask?

t_ch: 现在我到处都找不到神仙姐姐，只好暂作歇息。{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，还有什么事情吗？
t_en: But now I can't find her, so I'm having a breather. {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, is there anything else you want to ask?

t_ch: 您的打扮也和这里的男仙人女仙人差太远啦。冬生实在没法将{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}当作神仙看待。
t_en: You look so different from the celestial men and women here. I just can't see {F#Miss}{M#Mister} as a celestial.

t_ch: 神仙姐姐才不是迷信哩！在咱们那里，无论男女老幼都认得神仙姐姐，见她亲自施展过仙术，救了咱一村子的人！
t_en: She's not some old wives' tale! Everyone knows who she is where I'm from! I even saw her using celestial magic to save my entire village!

t_ch: 对呀，这里可是天外仙境，听说这里生活的都是神仙！
t_en: Oh yeah — This is an otherworldly realm. They say that all the celestials live here!

t_ch: 想和你聊聊天。
t_en: I want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 好呀，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}有什么想聊的话题吗？
t_en: Sure! {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, what do you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 跟我聊聊神仙姐姐吧。
t_en: Tell me about this celestial lady.

t_ch: 神仙姐姐？咱从小就听着神仙姐姐的故事入眠的。说来话长……
t_en: The celestial lady? I grew up with her stories as bedtime tales! It's a long story...

t_ch: 咱们村紧挨着一个叫赤泉山的地方。大家的日子都过的苦哈哈的，一年里吃不到一次大鸡腿。
t_en: Our village is next to a place called Redspring Peak. Our lives were really hard. We'd go an entire year without eating a single chicken drumstick.

t_ch: 穷乡僻壤嘛，一年到头，不是种些蔫巴巴的庄稼，就是去林子里打点兔子野鼠什么的，勉强糊口。
t_en: We were some backwater village. The only thing we did was plant crops that don't get enough water to grow, or hunt for hares and rats to keep ourselves fed.

t_ch: 偏生就是这样的苦日子，山里面还有妖孽横行，简直就是往苦水里怼盐巴，叫人活不下去哩。
t_en: On top of this tough life, we also have demons rampaging in the mountain. It's like rubbing salt in the wound. It made life really really difficult.

t_ch: 据说在咱出生前很久，有一年，天空里最北边的星星的位置突然亮了起来，据说当时照的夜空像白昼。
t_en: Legends say that long before I was born, the northernmost star in the sky suddenly shone so brightly that it turned night into day.

t_ch: 村里懂些天象的老人说，这是有星宿要下凡，来惩治妖孽哩。后来过了五六年，有一天，天上突然掉下一个蛋来！
t_en: Some older villagers who knew about the stars said a constellation was about to descend to rid of evil. And then, five or six years later, an egg suddenly fell from the sky!

t_ch: 蛋？
t_en: An egg?

t_ch: 故事里说是蛋，应该就是蛋了。这蛋裂开，里面就跳出了个可漂亮的姐姐哩。
t_en: The story said it was an egg, so it must've been an egg. This egg cracked open, and out came a beautiful lady.

t_ch: 神仙姐姐？
t_en: The celestial lady?

t_ch: 是啊。这蛋裂开，里面就跳出了个可漂亮的姐姐哩。
t_en: Yes. This egg cracked open and out popped a beautiful lady.

t_ch: 来旺说，他家太爷的太爷当时正在林子里打猎，第一个撞见了那景象。那颗蛋在地上生生犁出一条几十里长的山沟沟来，整片林子都烧起火来。
t_en: Laiwang said his great-grandfather's great-grandfather was hunting in the woods that day and was the first to see the scene. The egg cut out a ravine on the mountain that extended a few dozen miles, and the whole forest caught on fire.

t_ch: 村里人都觉得神仙姐姐漂亮可亲，就是脑袋有些问题。刚到村子里的时候，她连句整话都说不利索。当时村子里读书最多的长老给她起了个名儿……
t_en: Everyone thought the celestial lady was beautiful and nice, but there was something wrong with her head. When she first arrived, she couldn't even talk in a complete sentence. The most well-read elder gave her a name...

t_ch: …叫「蛋生」。后来有一天，神仙姐姐突然能开口说人话了，她说，你们还是管我叫「沐芳」吧。大家怕亵渎了仙人，就改口叫她「沐芳神女」啦。
t_en: ...Eggborn. Then one day, the celestial lady suddenly began to speak like a human. She said, "you can call me Mufang." Everyone was afraid of offending a celestial, so they called her "Goddess Mufang" instead.

t_ch: 呼…讲故事好累，你要是想听，下次再给你讲讲神仙姐姐其他的故事吧！
t_en: Phew... Storytelling is tiring. If you want to keep listening, I'll tell you more stories about her next time!

t_ch: 你是怎么来仙舟的？
t_en: How did you get to Xianzhou?

t_ch: 「仙舟」？你是说仙宫是吗？
t_en: Xianzhou? You mean the Celestial Hall?

t_ch: 有一天咱在山上砍柴。瞧见一艘大船——不是水上漂的那种，是…是飘在空中的，跟鸟毛一样轻——飘向咱们给神仙姐姐立的生祠那儿去了。
t_en: I was cutting wood in the mountains one day when I saw a giant ship. Not the type that floats on water, but... but one that floats in air like a feather. It floated towards the temple we built for the celestial lady.

t_ch: 咱寻思着一定是天上的仙人来接神仙姐姐啦！咱就跑啊跑，咱瞅见那艘船停着，船底开了个洞。
t_en: I thought it must be other celestials here to take her back! So I ran and ran, and saw that the ship had landed, and there was a hole at the bottom.

t_ch: 怪事，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，底下有洞的船还能开吗？不管这些啦，总之冬生就钻了进去。结果一觉醒来，就被带到了仙宫！
t_en: It's weird — {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, how can a ship still sail with a hole in it? Anyway, I climbed in and fell asleep. The next thing I knew, I was here at the Celestial Hall!

t_ch: 你怎么还能睡的着呢？
t_en: How could you even have fallen asleep?

t_ch: 咱跑了一路，也累了嘛。
t_en: I was tired from all that running.

t_ch: 真是仙缘不浅…
t_en: Your life is truly intertwined with the celestials...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，是吧。娘也说，冬生脑门子大，福气旺！
t_en: Heehee, isn't that right? My Ma even said that fortune favors me because I have a giant forehead!

t_ch: 再后来，有几个穿着盔甲的天兵发现了咱。他们给咱扎了一针，咱突然就能听懂仙人仙话啦。
t_en: And then, some celestial soldiers in armor discovered me. They gave me a poke with some needle, and I could suddenly understand celestial speech.

t_ch: 他们问了我几个问题，又给我换了身新衣裳。嘿嘿，这衣裳没洞洞，也不打补丁，真好看……
t_en: They asked me a few questions, and then dressed me in new clothes. Heehee, these clothes have no holes or patches on them. So pretty...

t_ch: 你在这里怎么照顾自己？
t_en: How do you take care of yourself here?

t_ch: 你别看咱小小年纪，咱可是经历过旱灾涝灾的！冬生可厉害了，三天不吃饭也饿不着。
t_en: I'm young, but I've lived through plenty of droughts and floods! I'm so awesome that I can go for three straight days without food!

t_ch: 不过这仙宫里的好心神仙，冬生有吃有住。那位「不夜侯」的说书先生听咱讲故事便愿意周济咱，真是慈悲心肠呀。
t_en: But thanks to the kind celestials in this Celestial Hall, I have food and a place to live. That storyteller mister at Sleepless Earl gives me money for listening to my stories. He's so good!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}别担心，咱听说「天上一天，人间一年」，时间紧迫，等咱找到神仙姐姐，讨到仙药之后，就得赶紧回去了。
t_en: Don't worry, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}. I heard that one day in heaven is a year on land. Time is running out. I'll go home once I find the celestial lady and get celestial medicine from her.

t_ch: 再见！咱继续去找神仙姐姐啦，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也一定能在仙舟上实现愿望的！
t_en: Bye-bye! I need to keep searching for the celestial lady. {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, your dreams will certainly come true as well in Xianzhou!

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 再见！咱继续去找神仙姐姐啦，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也一定能在仙舟上实现愿望的！
t_en: Bye-bye! I need to keep searching for the celestial lady. {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, your dreams will certainly come true as well in Xianzhou!

t_ch: 跟我聊聊神仙姐姐吧。
t_en: Tell me about this celestial lady.

t_ch: 好啊。很久很久以前，据说比神仙姐姐来之前还要久的时候，咱们村紧挨的赤泉山里据说住着一头叫「山君」的妖孽。
t_en: Sure. A long long long time ago, even before the celestial lady got here, the Redspring Peak next to my village had a demon called the Mountain Lord.

t_ch: 他发起怒来，土地都要裂开，泉眼儿也不出水。为了取悦他，乡亲们年年供奉，为他掘山开矿，还要献出亲生儿女充作山君的侍僮哩。
t_en: When he got angry, the earth would split open and springs go all dry. The village gave him tribute every year to appease him, work in mines for him, and even offered their children as the Mountain Lord's servants.

t_ch: 据说山君带走的那些孩子，都被变成了吃人的狼怪供他使唤。赤泉山周遭的几条村子的青壮汉子都想尽法子要除掉山君。
t_en: The kids that the Mountain Lord took away were turned into man-eating wolf monsters that answered his commands. All the grown men in the villages around the Redspring Peak were all bent on getting rid of the Mountain Lord.

t_ch: 但那家伙毕竟是妖怪。力大无穷，站起来足有三四个人高，铜铃般的眼睛，火灼灼的，一身铜皮铁骨。大家伙儿就算一齐上，也不过是白饶了许多性命。
t_en: But he was a demon after all. He was inhumanly strong, taller than several adults, with eyes big as bronze bells that flickered like fire, and really tough skin. Even if everyone attacked him at the same time, people would still get killed by him.

t_ch: 乡亲们都在那家伙的压迫下，过了很久的苦日子。后来嘛……
t_en: The villagers suffered for a long time under that demon. And then...

t_ch: 后来怎么了？
t_en: Then what?

t_ch: 这也跟神仙姐姐没关系。
t_en: This has nothing to do with the celestial lady.

t_ch: 后来神仙姐姐降临凡间。长老们就求她展示神通。好个神仙姐姐，听到乡亲们受苦拍案而起，提剑就进了赤泉山。
t_en: And then the celestial lady descended to our world and the elders begged her to use her divine magic. Lo and behold the celestial lady! Up she went with her sword when she heard about how the villagers had suffered, and she went into Redspring Peak.

t_ch: 过了一阵子，有一天大家突然瞧见赤泉山顶有电闪雷劈，风呼啦啦吹。要不了多久，整个赤泉山的山头突然塌了，以后都改叫「平顶山」了。
t_en: After a while, we suddenly saw a thunderstorm with terrible winds on Redspring Peak. Then the peak just completely collapsed, so we now call it "Flat-Top Peak."

t_ch: 从那以后，山君就再也不出来作祟了，大家也过上了一段时间的安生日子。
t_en: The Mountain Lord never came out to make trouble again, and everyone lived happily for a while.

t_ch: 怎么样，这个故事精彩吧？神仙姐姐的神迹可不止这些喔。
t_en: How is it? Was this story exciting? The celestial lady has done a lot more than this.

t_ch: 呼…讲故事好累，你要是想听，下次再给你讲其他神仙姐姐的故事吧！
t_en: Phew... storytelling is tiring. If you want to listen, I'll tell you more stories about her next time!

t_ch: 跟我聊聊神仙姐姐吧。
t_en: Tell me about this celestial lady.

t_ch: 好！虽然咱年纪不大，可是知道很多神仙姐姐的故事哦。咱想想，嗯…讲这个故事吧。
t_en: Sure! I'm young, but I know lots and lots of stories about the celestial lady. Let's see, hmm... I'll tell you this one then.

t_ch: 镇上有个富商，因为很喜欢做善事，大家都叫他周大善人。可是，老天爷不开眼，周大善人的孩子却是个天生的呆瓜，脑袋比牛好不了多少。
t_en: There was a wealthy merchant in town. Everyone called him Kind Zhou because he always helped people. But fate was mean to him. His son was born a dummy and had difficulties doing everything.

t_ch: 周大善人到处寻医问药，但是都没有人能治好他儿子。最后他听说了神仙姐姐的事迹，就求到了咱们村子里。
t_en: Kind Zhou asked everywhere for help, but no one could cure his son. Then he heard about what the celestial lady could do, and he made his way to our village.

t_ch: 神仙姐姐本来不打算帮忙，但她听了乡亲们的求告，又知道周大善人乐善好施，最后还是生了慈悲心。
t_en: She didn't want to help him at first, but she listened to everyone who put in a word for Kind Zhou about how he helps everyone, so she had mercy on him in the end.

t_ch: 据说她腾云驾雾回到那颗蛋掉下来的地方，炼了丹药，交给大善人，命他按时用药。
t_en: People say she flew on a cloud and returned to where the egg fell, made a medicine, gave it to the kind man, and told him to take it on time.

t_ch: 那个丹药果然不同凡响，周大善人家的傻孩子吃了不久，突然会开口叫爸爸了，后来长大还考取了功名。
t_en: That medicine was a miracle. After Kind Zhou's son took it, he suddenly said the word "Father." And when the son grew up, he even passed his exams with flying colors and became an official.

t_ch: 不仅如此，据说周大善人母鸡和大黄狗偷吃了那丹药，都变成了会说话的灵兽，也活了好多好多年！
t_en: Not just that, people say that Kind Zhou's chickens and dog stole the pellet, and became divine beasts that could talk. They even lived for many many years!

t_ch: 爸爸妈妈一直教咱，要像周大善人一样多做善事，这样咱和咱的阿菜都可以得道成仙！哎，也不知道阿菜现在怎么样了…阿菜是咱养的狗狗啦！
t_en: My parents always taught me to do lots of good deeds like Kind Zhou. That way, Veggie and I could become celestials too! Oh, I wonder how Veggie's doing... Veggie's my dog!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，神仙姐姐的故事是不是特别特别神奇？呼…讲故事好累，你要是想听，下次再给你讲其他神仙姐姐的故事吧！
t_en: Heehee, aren't the celestial lady's stories so amazingly? Phew, storytelling is hard work. If you want more, I'll tell you other stories about her next time!

t_ch: 跟我聊聊神仙姐姐吧。
t_en: Tell me about this celestial lady.

t_ch: 好的呀！虽然咱年纪不大，可是知道很多神仙姐姐的故事哦。唔…给你讲一个咱爸爸小时候的故事吧！
t_en: Okay! I'm young, but I know lots of stories about her. Umm... I'll tell you the story from when my Dad was little!

t_ch: 神仙姐姐当时住在镇上的大宅子里，经常给大家看病。很多人病得特别特别重，谁都治不好，但是神仙姐姐轻而易举就治好了。
t_en: The celestial lady was living in a big manor in town and treated lots of people's illnesses. Some of them were really really sick and no one could cure them, but she cured them really easily.

t_ch: 后来，也有很多很多其他地方的人跑来找神仙姐姐治病。他们走了很远很远的路，就是为了求神仙姐姐的一副仙药。神仙姐姐也从不让他们失望。
t_en: Then, lots and lots of people from other places came to see her for their issues. They'd walk for so long just to get medicine from her, and the celestial lady never disappointed them.

t_ch: 神仙姐姐的名气越来越大，连官府也知道了。当时有一个很坏很坏的县官，去逼问神仙姐姐仙药的秘密，神仙姐姐当然不会告诉他。他就把神仙姐姐关了起来。
t_en: She became so famous that even the government officials knew about her. There was a very very bad county official who tried to force her to tell him the secrets of her celestial medicine. Of course she wouldn't, so he locked her up!

t_ch: 当天晚上，有两个金甲门神突然出现在县衙前，轰隆隆转眼就把监狱给整个拆掉了！那些卫兵看到这一幕，根本不敢上前阻拦！
t_en: And that very night, two golden-armored door guardians suddenly appeared in front of the magistrate's court, and tore apart the entire jail! The guards who saw this were too scared to do anything!

t_ch: 神仙姐姐大摇大摆地走出了监狱，回到了家。从此以后，再也没人敢找她的麻烦了。
t_en: The celestial lady casually walked out of jail and went home. No one dared to trouble her again.

t_ch: 再后来，连县官都要听神仙姐姐的命令，连皇帝都要来镇上找神仙姐姐求药。咱就是在皇帝来为太子求药的那个冬天出生的！所以叫做冬生！
t_en: Then even the county official had to listen to the celestial lady. Even the Emperor came to town to ask for her miracle medicine for the crown prince! That's the very winter when I was born! My name, Dongsheng, means "winterborn"!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，神仙姐姐的故事是不是特别特别神奇？呼…讲故事好累，你要是想听，下次再给你讲其他神仙姐姐的故事吧！
t_en: Heehee, aren't the celestial lady's stories so amazingly? Phew, storytelling is hard work. If you want more, I'll tell you other stories about her next time!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，又见到你啦！是有什么事要找咱吗？
t_en: {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, we meet again! What can I do for you this time?

t_ch: 哎，这仙舟中处处都是历史的痕迹，可仙舟民却不知道它们的价值。哎……
t_en: Ah, there're traces of history everywhere you look aboard Xianzhou, but Xianzhou natives don't even know these things' true value. *sigh*...

t_ch: 怎么唉声叹气的？
t_en: What's with all the sighing?

t_ch: 哎…说了你也不懂…嗯？这装束…你也是化外民吗？那倒是可以跟你说道说道。
t_en: *sigh* ...You wouldn't get it even if I told you. Your outfit... are you an Outworlder too? Then I guess we can talk a little about it.

t_ch: 仙舟民不知道它们的价值，哎…
t_en: The Xianzhou natives don't even know its true value, sigh...

t_ch: 到底是谁在学我说话？哦，我看你这化外民装束，我明白了，你也受不了仙舟民了对不对？
t_en: Who's copying how I talk? Oh, you're wearing Outworlder clothes. I get it, I get it... You can't bear these Xianzhou natives either, right?

t_ch: 简直受不了。
t_en: Can't stand it.

t_ch: 对啊！仙舟民自恃长寿，对历史文物丝毫不懂得珍惜…等等，你不会是在套我的话吧？哎，算了，我也不与你计较。
t_en: Right!? Because of their longevity, they don't even have the smallest care about historical relics... hang on, are you trying to pump a confession out of me? Ack, forget about it. I don't have any beef with you.

t_ch: 你是说哪一方面呢？
t_en: In what way?

t_ch: 我还以为遇上了同道中人，结果就只是遇见了一位复读机。算了，我也不与你计较了，我是在说仙舟历史文化的事情。
t_en: I thought I met a kindred spirit, but you're just a bot who repeats everything. Forget about it. I don't have any beef with you. I'm just talking about the history and culture of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 仙舟民和我们不一样，随便找几个人问问都是活了有几百岁。所以化外民心中久远的历史，不过是人家心目中的「童年回忆」。
t_en: The Xianzhou natives are different from us. Ask any of them, and you'll find they've lived for centuries. What we Outworlders consider ancient history is actually just a childhood memory for them.

t_ch: 这也就导致仙舟上的「历史学」和我们短生种理解中的「历史学」根本不是一个东西…我就这么说吧，他们缺乏对历史的敬畏心！
t_en: This means the study of "history" on Xianzhou is completely different from what we consider as "history"... or put simply, they lack respect for the past!

t_ch: 通常考古学家们得从旧时代的器皿、碑铭中推测联想过去发生了什么，这仙舟上各种东西更新换代太快了，几百年的东西说扔掉就扔掉，真是孰不可忍！
t_en: Archaeologists often speculate on the past by studying utensils and inscriptions from antiquity. But on Xianzhou, everything is perpetually being upgraded. They simply chuck out centuries-old antiques... it's just awful.

t_ch: 怎么？你也对仙舟史研究感兴趣，忘了自我介绍了，我是博识学会的陶德，要是对仙舟史感兴趣，咱们可以再探讨探讨。
t_en: Huh? You're also a keen student of Xianzhou history? Apologies, I haven't even introduced myself yet: I'm Todd from the Intelligentsia Guild. We can discuss things a little more if you're interested in Xianzhou history.

t_ch: 就是随便聊聊天。
t_en: Just a casual chat.

t_ch: 聊天？好啊，我正好歇歇脑子。况且和仙舟上不同的人聊天也算是我研究的一部分。
t_en: Chat? Alright, I can use a break now anyway. Besides, chatting with different people aboard Xianzhou also forms part of my research.

t_ch: 博识学会是做什么的？
t_en: What does the Intelligentsia Guild do?

t_ch: 我虽然是博识学会的学者，但是我对博识学会的描述也是片面的，不客观的。这一点，我希望你能把它当作前提来看待。
t_en: I may be a student there, but my opinion of the Intelligentsia Guild is bound to be biased. So take this with a grain of salt.

t_ch: 简而言之，博识学会是与星际和平公司共生的学术组织。倡导的理念是：「一切知识必须如货币般流通」。
t_en: Put simply, the Intelligentsia Guild is an academic organization in symbiosis with the Interastral Peace Corporation. Our motto is "All knowledge must be circulated as freely as currency."

t_ch: 学会的学士们遍历宇宙，搜集一切有价值的知识，然后用所得知识与其他学士们交换知识，为的是寻求真正的无价之宝。
t_en: Our scholars traverse the universe collecting all knowledge of value, and trading learnings with other scholars in the hope of attaining priceless wisdom.

t_ch: 说这么多你也理解不了，你只用知道，我们便是交易知识取得真知的团体就行了。
t_en: It's hard to explain, but basically, all you need to know is that we're a group that trades knowledge to gain true wisdom.

t_ch: 仙舟人不研究历史吗？
t_en: Don't the Xianzhou natives research history?

t_ch: 倒不是不研究。只是仙舟人所谓的「历史学」，和我们短生种所谓「历史学」根本就不是同一个概念。
t_en: It's not that they don't study it. It's just that what they call "history" is a completely different concept from what our short-life species consider to be history.

t_ch: 道理也很简单。你会把你小时候去过、长大后被荒废的剧场，当做「考古学」的遗址吗？对于仙舟人来说，我们眼中久远的历史，不过是他们的童年回忆罢了。
t_en: It's a simple concept. Would you consider the abandoned theater that you used to visit as a child as some kind of archaeological ruin? What we consider to be ancient history is merely a childhood memory for the long-lived Xianzhou natives.

t_ch: 所以，仙舟人不太珍视他们的古迹，就如同我们不太珍视自己城市里的旧房子。他们也不太重视整理成文史，因为「历史」还活在每个当事人的脑中。
t_en: That's why the Xianzhou natives don't really value their own cultural relics, just as we don't really care about dilapidated buildings back home. They also don't really get the concept of written history, because "history" still lives within their memories.

t_ch: 但是万物终有尽头，仙舟人也不例外。作为博识学会的历史学家，我岂能坐视不管，他们不在乎，我在乎，我来为银河灿烂星史执笔。
t_en: But everything must come to an eventual end one day, and the Xianzhou natives are no exception. As a student of the Intelligentsia Guild, how can I sit idly by? They don't care, but I do — so I'm here to record the magnificence of our cosmos's history.

t_ch: 你做这些的意义是什么？
t_en: What's your purpose behind doing all of this?

t_ch: 你怎么说话跟我读书时的导师似的？哎，搞得我又想起以前，每次都要给我关心的学术课题编一个实际作用……
t_en: You sound just like one of my tutors! *sigh* That reminds me of school and having to find a practical purpose for every academic topic I wanted to write about...

t_ch: 咳咳，哼…不过，研究仙舟史当然是有用的。毕竟我们都知道，仙舟上藏着永生的秘密。即便仙舟人可以守口如瓶，但「历史」始终是不会沉默的。
t_en: Cough, ahem... but of course, it's useful to study the history of Xianzhou. After all, we all know that the secret of immortality lies hidden somewhere here. The Xianzhou natives might be able to keep their secret, but history shall never be kept silent.

t_ch: 只要能从断壁残垣和废纸残章里拼凑出足够多的蛛丝马迹，也许就能引领我们找到这件仙舟上最大的秘密……
t_en: So long as we can piece together enough clues from the rubble and scraps around us, perhaps it'll lead us to finding that legendary secret here on Xianzhou...

t_ch: 没别的事了，你忙吧。
t_en: That's all, I can see you're busy.

t_ch: 嗯，我们各忙各的去吧。如果你看到了什么有趣的历史遗存，记得通知我一声。
t_en: Yes, let us part ways here. If you see any interesting historical relics, give me a holler.

t_ch: 没什么正事，你忙吧。
t_en: It's nothing, really. I can see you're busy.

t_ch: 嗯，我们各忙各的去吧。如果你看到了什么有趣的历史遗存，记得通知我一声。
t_en: Yes, let us part ways here. If you see any interesting historical relics, give me a holler.

t_ch: 是你呀？看你这张脸就能感觉出来，咱俩都是劳碌命。
t_en: It's you. I can tell just by looking at your face that you're a workaholic — just like me.

t_ch: 我现在要么被巨量的一手资料淹没，要么就在仙舟上到处调查走访，风里来雨里去，好不容易才能休息这么一会儿。
t_en: I'm either swamped by primary sources or running around everywhere on Xianzhou for research purposes. With my hectic schedule, it's a miracle that I ever find time like this to rest.

t_ch: 博识学会的规矩是用知识交换知识，你最好准备了些我感兴趣的东西。所以，今天有什么事要找我吗？
t_en: The Intelligentsia Guild always trades knowledge for knowledge, and that's our guiding principle. You'd better prepare something interesting for me. So what can I do for you today?

t_ch: 您好，鹤运速递为您服务，请问您预约了寄件吗？
t_en: Heron Express, at your service! Have you made a delivery booking with us?

t_ch: 你是哪位？
t_en: Who are you again?

t_ch: 我是鹤运物流专员，梓桥。
t_en: I'm Ziqiao, a logistics specialist from Heron Express.

t_ch: 寄件？
t_en: Delivery?

t_ch: 没错，寄件请预约鹤运速递～我是鹤运物流专员，梓桥。
t_en: That's right. For deliveries, please book a courier with Heron Express. I'm a Heron Express Specialist — Ziqiao's my name.

t_ch: 您如果有要寄送的货品，请提前在智能终端上打开鹤运物流的应用，输入寄件信息。上门收件的话等物流机巧鸟通知，出门寄件的话就带着货品来这里。
t_en: If you've got a delivery to send, please enter your shipping info via our Heron Express app. If you're waiting to receive a delivery, please wait for our courier cycranes' notification. If you'd like to send a delivery, please bring it to our office.

t_ch: 仙舟上到处都是物流机巧鸟。
t_en: Courier cycranes are everywhere aboard the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 哈哈，仙舟上现在已经离不开鹤运物流了，吃饭点餐、网络购物、同城速递…可以说，要是没有咱们鹤运物流，这仙舟多半是难以运转了。
t_en: Haha, Xianzhou relies heavily on Heron Express Specialists: Delivering meals, online shopping, within-city deliveries... It'd be very difficult to operate Xianzhou without Heron Express.

t_ch: 你别看很多人说自己可以待在家里大门不出、二门不迈，要是没有快递和外卖，你说他能待得下去吗？绝对待不下去的。
t_en: Don't listen to all those claims about being able to stay indoors here. Without courier deliveries, do you really think anyone could stand staying in? Nobody could.

t_ch: 在路边看到了很多的快递箱。
t_en: I saw a lot of parcels outside.

t_ch: 这件事让我一直有些头疼，有一些是因为机巧鸟故障中途暂时搁置，有些则是因为用户填写了错误的寄件信息，再也联系不上寄件人，暂时搁置的。
t_en: This always gives me a headache. Some were put on hold due to cycrane failure; some were because the customer filled out their contact details incorrectly, so we couldn't get in touch with them.

t_ch: 虽然说处理这些废置的快递箱也是我的工作，但是实在是太多了，如果实在找不到失主，我们也只能慈善处理了。
t_en: It may be my job to deal with these abandoned parcels, but there're just too many. If we can't track down the owner, we end up donating things to charity.

t_ch: 话又说回来，我其实还在工作呢。您要是没什么想要寄的，就记得咱们鹤运物流的服务啊。我们的口号是「鹤运速递，啥都能寄」。
t_en: But then again, I'm still on the clock right now. If you don't have anything to send right now, keep Heron Express in mind for next time. Our slogan is: Heron Express — We Mail Anything.

t_ch: 要是你没什么别的事儿，我就接着忙了。
t_en: If there's nothing else, I'm getting back to work.

t_ch: 还想再接着聊聊。
t_en: There're still some things I'd like to talk about.

t_ch: 好啊，就只是聊聊工作的话，我不太介意。
t_en: Alright. I don't mind if we're just talking about work.

t_ch: 你平时都做些什么？
t_en: What do you usually get up to around here?

t_ch: 其实很多人都会问我这样的问题。毕竟仙舟上到处都是鹤运物流的机巧鸟，又需要人力去做些什么呢？
t_en: I get asked that a lot, actually. After all, with this many cycranes aboard the Xianzhou, what do you need manual labor like me for?

t_ch: 实际上，鹤运物流的很多工作依然需要人来进行。譬如检查货物、调配机巧鸟线路、定期检修机巧鸟，再说你也看到街边那些快递箱了……
t_en: In actual fact, much of our work relies on human workforce: Inspecting goods, deploying cycrane routes, cycrane maintenance... plus all those pesky abandoned boxes you've seen on the streets...

t_ch: 对了，还有一项工作是必须由人类来负责，且几百年内不会改变的，那就是受理客户投诉。
t_en: Oh yeah — there's another job that can only be done manually, and that's never going to change: Handling customer service complaints.

t_ch: 当真啥都能寄吗？
t_en: Is it true that you can really mail anything?

t_ch: 当然了。我们的口号就是「鹤运速递，啥都能寄」，不过最终的解释权还得在我们这里。
t_en: Sure. Our slogan is "Heron Express — We Mail Anything," but we reserve the right of final interpretation.

t_ch: 如果您需要运送的物品特别敏感或者特别重要，我们通常不建议您使用物流机器人，我们这里建议您寻找一个镖局——带有武装押运的物流企业。
t_en: We usually recommend that you avoid using robots if you're sending anything fragile or important. Instead, we recommend that you hire an armed escort service.

t_ch: 您如果有什么想邮寄的东西，正式下单之前，请阅读寄送规范。
t_en: Before you finalize your order with us, please make sure you read our shipping regulations.

t_ch: 仙舟的物流真是方便呐。
t_en: Logistics on Xianzhou is really something else.

t_ch: 对吧？从你们化外民的视角来看，比仙舟民本身要清楚得多。我老是听一些本地老害说「仙舟是一成不变，死气沉沉的」，我可不同意。
t_en: I know, right? You Outworlders have a much clearer perspective on this stuff than the Xianzhou natives. I'm always hearing old cranks say "Xianzhou is lifeless and never changes," but I disagree.

t_ch: 最直观的感受，就是近二百年来我越来越繁忙了。您能想象吗？在一百五十年前，星槎海的繁华区，每天只会飞过六千四百多架次货运机巧鸟。
t_en: It's just a hunch, but I've grown busier and busier these past two hundred years. Can you imagine? Around <unbreak>150</unbreak> years ago, down at the busy Starskiff Haven zone, we'd only be able to handle over <unbreak>6400</unbreak> cycrane deliveries per day.

t_ch: 而就在昨天，星槎海的繁华区上空飞过了…我看一下数据…七万架次——昨天还是工作日。而这只是短短一百五十年间发生的变化。
t_en: But just yesterday, there were... let me see... over <unbreak>70,000</unbreak> deployments over Starskiff Haven! And that's on a work day! Such a huge change in just <unbreak>150</unbreak> years.

t_ch: 不留心看不见，你可真仔细。
t_en: That's easy to miss if you're not paying attention. You've got quite the eye.

t_ch: 物流行业真是越来越重要了。
t_en: The logistics industry is becoming more and more important these days.

t_ch: 您一定也能发现了，城市越发达、生活越便捷，就越是依赖货运系统的支撑。如今的仙舟可根本承受不起物流系统停摆，咱的责任也更重了。
t_en: You must also find that the more developed and convenient a city becomes, the more its citizens come to rely on logistical support. Today, Xianzhou can't afford to pause onboard logistics, and our burden is even heavier.

t_ch: 仙舟是一个巨人，物流系统是她的血管，我们物流专员负责维系她的心跳。只要想到这里，我就充满了「活着」的充实感。
t_en: Xianzhou is a massive giant, and our logistics system is its blood vessels. Our couriers keep its heart beating. Whenever I think of this, I'm filled with the feeling of being truly alive.

t_ch: 没别的事情了，辛苦了。
t_en: That's all. Keep up the good work.

t_ch: 您路上小心。请记住我们的口号，「鹤运速递，啥都能寄」。
t_en: Be careful out there, and remember — our slogan is "Heron Express — We Mail Anything."

t_ch: 没别的事情了，辛苦了。
t_en: That's all. Keep up the good work.

t_ch: 您路上小心。请记住我们的口号，「鹤运速递，啥都能寄」。
t_en: Be careful out there, and remember — our slogan is "Heron Express — We Mail Anything."

t_ch: 您好…哦，我记得您。这次您预约了寄件吗？
t_en: Oh, hi... I remember you. Have you booked a delivery with us?

t_ch: 中小包裹，当日可达。大的包裹，也就需要两到三天。您有什么需求，敬请吩咐。
t_en: We can do same-day delivery for small and medium packages. Big ones take two to three days, though. If you have any other requirements, please let us know.

t_ch: 这位行人，一定要注意交通安全呀。
t_en: Citizen! Please pay attention to where you're stepping.

t_ch: 你是哪位？
t_en: Who are you again?

t_ch: 我叫檀檀，是天舶司的航速测试员。
t_en: The name's Tantan, and I'm a speed tester for the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 我正在这里执勤，看看有没有人超速驾驶星槎，保障交通安全。
t_en: I'm on duty here right now, making sure no one is speeding with their starskiffs and ensuring traffic safety.

t_ch: 你在干什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 我正在这里执勤，看看有没有人超速驾驶星槎，保障交通安全。
t_en: I'm on duty here right now, making sure no one is speeding with their starskiffs and ensuring traffic safety.

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍…我叫檀檀，是天舶司的航速测试员。
t_en: Oh, I forgot to introduce myself... My name's Tantan, and I'm a speed tester for the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 在人一生中所有可能遭遇的飞来横祸中，交通事故是最常见也最致命的，所以千万不可以对交通安全放松警惕哦。
t_en: Of all the accidents that can happen in life, traffic accidents are the most common and most deadly. So please don't let your guard down when it comes to traffic safety.

t_ch: 谢谢提醒，我会注意的。
t_en: Thanks for the reminder. I'll keep that in mind.

t_ch: 这都是我应该做的。我的梦想就是，仙舟有朝一日可以没有交通事故。
t_en: It's my job. I dream that one day, Xianzhou will be free of any crashes.

t_ch: 没事，我身强体壮，很扛撞的。
t_en: No worries. I'm built differently.

t_ch: 别说这种蠢话。无论是仙舟人还是化外民，生命都只有一次。可绝不要因为这种无聊的事而白白浪费掉哦。
t_en: Nonsense. You only get one chance at life, whether you're a Xianzhou native or an Outworlder. Don't waste it on such a trivial matter.

t_ch: 总而言之，「一秒钟事故，一千年痛苦」，可千万要记在心里。
t_en: Remember: Accidents only take one second to happen, but the pain can last a thousand years. Never forget that.

t_ch: 怎么，你还有什么事要咨询吗，如果是了解交通安全知识，随时都可以。
t_en: Huh? Is there anything else you need assistance with? If it's anything about traffic safety, feel free to ask me any time.

t_ch: 我还有些想问的。
t_en: There's something else I want to ask.

t_ch: 随时准备好解答市民疑惑，这也是天舶司测速员的责任，你问吧。
t_en: I'm always ready to assuage the public's doubts. That's part of my duty as a speed tester at the Sky-Faring Commission as well. Just ask away.

t_ch: 测速员平时都做些什么？
t_en: What do speed testers usually do?

t_ch: 嗯？你对我平时的工作感兴趣吗？
t_en: Mmm? Are you interested in my everyday work?

t_ch: 我平时的工作还蛮无聊的，就是站在这里为往来的星槎测速。如果发现超速星槎，就要把他拦下来狠狠普法。
t_en: My everyday job is pretty boring. I just stand here and measure the speeds of every starskiff going past. If I catch anyone speeding, I stop them and firmly educate them about the law.

t_ch: 这项工作很无聊，但非常重要，每年都有很多仙舟人，自以为拥有漫长的寿命，却因为危险驾驶星槎而早早离世。
t_en: This job may be boring, but it's extremely important. Every year, many Xianzhou natives die a premature death due to dangerous driving — despite their natural longevity.

t_ch: 不过无论多么无聊的工作，只要有意义，那就比周末的懒觉还令人愉快，不是吗？
t_en: But no matter how boring the job might be, the fulfillment alone can still be more enjoyable than just lazily sleeping in on the weekends. Wouldn't you say so?

t_ch: 有很多不遵守交通规则的人吗？
t_en: Do many people speed?

t_ch: 其实仙舟本地人都还好吧…啊，没有冒犯你的意思。只是大家都活得比较久，相对而言比较稳重，不会把车开得太快。
t_en: Actually, Xianzhou natives are relatively decent at driving... ahem, no offense intended. People here can live a very long life and are relatively calm, so they don't drive too fast.

t_ch: 但也有那种不遵守交通的长生种，觉得自己活得足够久了，无论做什么都不会出事，就开着星槎狂飙突进…结果就…哎，多可惜啊。
t_en: But there are also some long-life species that don't obey the speed limit. They feel like they've lived for so long that nothing could possibly happen to them, so they speed like crazy in their starskiffs... resulting in rather... uh, unfortunate situations.

t_ch: 我最近在想，要不然开个讲座，专门给大家普及交通安全知识怎么样？
t_en: I've been thinking — how about I give a professional lecture about traffic safety for the public?

t_ch: 讲座的名字我都想好了，就叫《檀檀谈谈》吧，或者叫《檀檀讲坛》！你觉得如何？
t_en: I've come up with a name for it already: Tantan's Tenet! Or In Tandem With Tantan. What do you think?

t_ch: 听起来有点拗口…
t_en: A bit of a tongue-twister...

t_ch: 唔…是有点拗口，也许可以更顺口一点…有了！
t_en: Hmm... you're not wrong. Maybe there are other names that could roll off the tongue a little easier.

t_ch: 也许可以更直白一点？
t_en: Perhaps it could be a bit more straightforward?

t_ch: 有道理…更直白一点…有了！
t_en: Makes sense... more straightforward... got it!

t_ch: 既然我的名字是檀檀，讲座又是关于交通安全的，那这个讲座就叫做……
t_en: Since my name's Tantan and it's a lecture about traffic safety, why don't I call it...

t_ch: 《檀檀安全》！哎呀，真不错。过段时间等我闲下来，就去天舶司做一下申请吧……
t_en: Traffic Safety with Tantan! Ah, it's perfect. Once I get some free time, I'll report back to the Sky-Faring Commission and put in a submission for it...

t_ch: 平时的工作很忙吗？
t_en: Do you get very busy at work?

t_ch: 那可太忙了。在这里为星槎测速是一件事，高峰期统计往来星槎数量是一件事、为路过行人普法…就像现在这样…也是一件事…哎，工作总也做不完。
t_en: Way too busy. It's one thing to measure every starskiff's speed, but it's another to count the number of starskiffs in rush-hour traffic. And I have to enforce the law for each case... like I am right now. It's just another thing to do. My job is never done.

t_ch: 有时候遇到复杂的状况…比如高峰期有人超速后肇事逃逸…那真的是分身乏术。虽然我如此热爱这份工作，但也偶尔会觉得这份工作太难了。
t_en: Sometimes, things get really complicated... like a hit-and-run during rush-hour traffic... If only I have a clone to get everything done. As much as I love this job, it's sometimes just too hard to handle.

t_ch: 所以，我这里其实也会找一些「交通志愿者」来帮忙。怎么样，有时间的话，要不要和我一起为仙舟做贡献呢？当然啦，说是「志愿者」，但也不会让你做白工的。
t_en: That's why I've found a few traffic volunteers to help me out here. Say, if you've got the spare time, would you like to contribute to the peace and order aboard Xianzhou? Naturally, it's just a volunteer position but I won't be sending you home empty-handed.

t_ch: 平时的工作有危险吗？
t_en: Does it get very dangerous?

t_ch: 我这份工作，算是执法部门里比较安全的了吧。没什么恶性犯罪，也不会面对什么真正的坏人……
t_en: In law enforcement circles, my job is pretty safe. There's no violent crime, and no truly bad crooks...

t_ch: 如果有人肇事逃逸，我倒是会开着星槎去追。要说危险，那就是我能遇到的最危险的事了吧。
t_en: If there's a hit-and-run, I'll give chase in my starskiff. I guess if you call that dangerous, then that's as dangerous as it gets.

t_ch: 看你的样子，应该不是等闲之辈。哈哈，我这种「危险」在你听来大概真的不算什么吧。
t_en: Judging by your appearance, you're no ordinary citizen. Haha, I bet my sense of "danger" doesn't really sound like much to you.

t_ch: 的确，我的工作就是这样缺乏趣味。但是整个仙舟的交通系统想要运转顺畅，就需要千千万万个我这样的人，去做这样无聊的工作。
t_en: Indeed, my work is pretty boring. But if the entire transport network of Xianzhou wants to function correctly, then it needs thousands of people like me to carry out these boring duties.

t_ch: 没什么事了…
t_en: I have nothing else...

t_ch: 嗯，那再见~路上小心，一定要注意交通安全！…哦对了，如果想做「交通志愿者」，记得来找我哦。
t_en: So long then~ Take care driving out there, and pay attention to the traffic! ...Oh yeah, if you do ever want to be a traffic volunteer, come find me.

t_ch: 没什么事了…
t_en: I have nothing else...

t_ch: 嗯，那再见~路上小心，一定要注意交通安全！…哦对了，如果想做「交通志愿者」，记得来找我哦。
t_en: So long then~ Take care driving out there, and pay attention to the traffic! ...Oh yeah, if you do ever want to be a traffic volunteer, come find me.

t_ch: 「一秒钟事故，一千年痛苦。」珍惜生命，遵守交通规则…啊，是你呀。
t_en: "Accidents only take one second to happen, but the pain can last a thousand years." Cherish life, and obey the law — Oh, it's you again.

t_ch: 今天找我有什么事吗？尽量长话短说哦，我现在还是有点忙的。
t_en: What can I do for you today? And make it snappy, please — I'm kinda busy today.

t_ch: 哎，虽说我是有旅费报销的，但是报销以外的部分我就发愁了呀。难道说我这样有正式工作的人，还得找门儿副业营生吗……
t_en: *sigh* My travel expenses can be reimbursed. It's the expenses that can't be reimbursed that annoy me. Am I going to have to moonlight even when I have a proper job...?

t_ch: 嘿，你也是跟我一样，现在滞留仙舟了吗？
t_en: Heh, are you stranded on Xianzhou just like me?

t_ch: 我也滞留了。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 我一看你就是！我有个事想和你打听一下……
t_en: I knew it! I have something to ask you...

t_ch: 我可是土生土长的老仙舟人了！
t_en: I am a true Xianzhou native, born and raised!

t_ch: 你脸上就差写着「化外民」三个字了，别闹了，我有个事想和你打听一下……
t_en: You've got "Outworlder" written all over your face. Jokes aside, I have something to ask you...

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍了，我叫艾德嘉，是来仙舟出差的商业法律顾问，现在暂时滞留仙舟。
t_en: Oh, I forgot to introduce myself. I am Edelia. I came to Xianzhou as a commercial legal adviser on a business trip, and am stranded here for a while.

t_ch: 我想和你打听一下，有什么化外民可以挣钱生活的门路吗？或者说，你平时靠什么养活自己？
t_en: I wanna ask if there is a way for Outworlders to make a living here? Rather, how do you usually earn money?

t_ch: 我在仙舟，上面有人…
t_en: I have someone on Xianzhou...

t_ch: 上面有人？你是想说有在仙舟，有帮你周转的朋友对吧？
t_en: Someone? You mean to say you have a friend on Xianzhou who can transfer money to you?

t_ch: 那可真好啊！常听仙舟人说，这就叫「海内存知己，天涯若比邻」吧。
t_en: Wonderful! I always hear the Xianzhou natives say "even across the heavens, the like-minded cross paths." This must be it.

t_ch: 我主要是做工具人…
t_en: I mainly work as a grunt...

t_ch: 原来如此。看来你也是个苦劳人啊…不过现在这种零碎的工作也不好找了。
t_en: I see. You are an individual of labor too... But it's hard to find even the most menial of jobs now.

t_ch: 其实我富得流油。
t_en: I actually have more wealth than you can imagine.

t_ch: 哦，原来是这样啊，早知道就不问你了……
t_en: Oh, is that so? I should've known better than to ask you...

t_ch: 我因为工作的关系被派遣到仙舟上出差，结果前段时间因为玉界门封锁，暂时没办法离开仙舟…原本我滞留在这儿的原因是这样的。
t_en: I was dispatched to Xianzhou because of work, but have been unable to leave since the Jade Gate's closure... That's the main reason for my situation.

t_ch: 但是现在我听说了些消息，改变主意了，并不想着急马上回去…这里毕竟是「仙舟」，藏着人人都感兴趣的秘密，你是「化外民」，也懂的吧？
t_en: But I've changed my mind after hearing certain information, and am not in a hurry to leave now... A secret that everyone is interested in is hiding in Xianzhou. Surely you know this, even as an Outworlder?

t_ch: 长生不老？
t_en: Immortality?

t_ch: 嘘！这可不是在客栈门口随便谈论的话题。
t_en: Shh! This isn't something that should be casually discussed in front of an inn.

t_ch: 我不太懂，您说说。
t_en: I don't get your drift. Please elaborate.

t_ch: 嗨，真是揣着聪明装糊涂。不过嘛…这里也不太适合谈论这个话题。
t_en: *sigh* You sure know how to play dumb. Then again... it's not something to be discussed openly here.

t_ch: 贪心长生的外来者，会被天舶司劝返，重罪者还有牢狱之灾。
t_en: Outworlders who covet immortality will be... peacefully deported by the Sky-Faring Commission. Hardened offenders will face prison time.

t_ch: 喏，这个送给你吧，当做封口费了，可不许出去乱说哦。
t_en: Here, this is for you. To keep your mouth shut. Don't breathe a word of this to anyone.

t_ch: 为了在仙舟待得更久一些，我待会儿还得找点差事做才行。你还有什么事要聊的吗？
t_en: I'll have to find something to do later to stay longer on Xianzhou. Do you have anything else to ask?

t_ch: 想随便聊聊。
t_en: Just a casual chat.

t_ch: 嗯，那就随便聊聊吧，反正一时半会儿我也不会很忙。
t_en: Sure, a casual chat it is then. It's not like I've got anywhere else to be.

t_ch: 打听到什么长生消息了？
t_en: Did you get any information on immortality?

t_ch: …你可真敢问啊，仙舟有仙舟的规矩，我们不该高声谈论。
t_en: ...You sure ask hard-hitting questions. Xianzhou has rules — we should not be talking about this in public.

t_ch: 不过，这也不算是什么不为人知的秘密。你知道这建木是有多引人注目吗？在「罗浮」仙舟上，任何人找一个高处便能清晰地望见它。
t_en: Then again, this isn't some clandestine secret. Do you realize how much of an eyecatcher the Ambrosial Arbor is? Anyone can find high ground on the Xianzhou Luofu and see it clear as day.

t_ch: 就算这里人人守口如瓶，那巨大的建木可不会沉默，它很快会告诉我们秘密到底是什么，玉界门的封锁就是一个信号。
t_en: Even if everyone's traps are sealed shut, that giant Ambrosial Arbor won't stay silent. It will soon tell us what the secret is. The Jade Gate's closure is the first clue.

t_ch: 现在都在靠什么赚钱？
t_en: What do you do to make money now?

t_ch: 浥尘客栈的老板知道我的情况之后，说是能给我介绍一些营生的手段。
t_en: Once the boss of the Petrichor Inn knew of my plight, they promised to introduce some jobs to me.

t_ch: 比如之前有位化外民老板有份兼职，让我给一些热门短视频刷评论。这个很简单没有什么门槛，我就试着做了做。
t_en: An Outworlder business owner had me write fake comments for short videos as a part-time gig. It was simple and entry-level, so I gave it a shot.

t_ch: 我在每个视频下面都发「孩子很喜欢，敏感肌也能用」，数量还不少。
t_en: Under each video, I wrote, "Children love this, great for sensitive skin too." I did that for LOTS of videos.

t_ch: 结果后来那位老板被地衡司带走了，也没有给我把工资结了，还蛮亏的。
t_en: In the end, that business owner was taken away by the Realm-Keeping Commission, and I didn't get paid. All that work down the drain.

t_ch: 接下来有什么计划？
t_en: What plans do you have next?

t_ch: 接下来？就和现在一样吧，做一些鸡零狗碎的工作，然后就是等待吧，我感觉这长生之谜就是纸团里的火，是包不住的……
t_en: Next? No different from now, I guess. Doing menial jobs, and just waiting. I feel that this puzzle to immortality is like trying to contain fire with paper — it can't be kept under wraps forever...

t_ch: 也许我有机会变成长生种，那个时候再做打算也不错…不过到那时，仙舟未必能让我离开吧。
t_en: Perhaps I'll get the chance to become long-life species. I can still make my decision when that time comes... then again, Xianzhou might not necessarily let me leave then.

t_ch: 你为什么想长生不老呢？
t_en: Why do you want to become immortal?

t_ch: 别说的你好像不感兴趣一样。
t_en: You say it like you were not interested in immortality at all.

t_ch: 感觉这辈子都没有机会了。
t_en: I don't think I'll ever get that chance.

t_ch: 我也不是死脑筋，要是真没有机会，我会走的。
t_en: I'm not that obsessed with the notion. If it's really not possible, I'll just leave.

t_ch: 我已经见识过这宇宙有多广袤了，这辈子太短了，不足够满足我。
t_en: I have witnessed the vastness of the cosmos. One lifetime is too short to satisfy my curiosity.

t_ch: 如果能有无限的寿命，我想走遍整片星空，你能理解我吧。
t_en: If I had infinite life, I would travel through the entirety of the universe. You get that, don't you?

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 好，那你路上小心。之后要是遇到什么适合我的工作，可以介绍给我。要是听到什么长生之秘…你懂的，别独占秘密呀。
t_en: All right, be careful on your travels. If you find any work I can do, please let me know. If you hear anything about the secret to immortality... you know, don't hog it all.

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 好，那你路上小心。之后要是遇到什么适合我的工作，可以介绍给我。要是听到什么长生之秘…你懂的，别独占秘密呀。
t_en: All right, be careful on your travels. If you find any work I can do, please let me know. If you hear anything about the secret to immortality... you know, don't hog it all.

t_ch: 听说这浥尘客栈的老板说，以前这家店的前身叫作「游云逆旅」，很少有人能完整念对，结果那家旅店就因为这名字开倒闭了。
t_en: I've heard from the Petrichor Inn owner that this shop used to be called the "Cloud-Striding Hotel for Travelers Near and Far," and it eventually went out of business because its name was such a mouthful.

t_ch: 仙舟人喜欢把这些陈芝麻烂谷子的旧事归因到一件小事，以此证明「以小见大」，我个人实在是不好评价。
t_en: The Xianzhou natives love attributing past events to trivial matters to show that "everything is in the details," but I reserve my judgment for that.

t_ch: 闲话听了一堆，长生秘法却什么都没打听到…说这些干嘛，你找我有什么事儿吗？
t_en: I've heard plenty of gossip, but nothing about the secret to immortality... Why are you talking about these? Do you have business with me?

t_ch: 危机过去了，你找到工作了吗？
t_en: Now that the crisis is over, have you found a job?

t_ch: 你别哪壶不开提哪壶嘛…老实说建木的影响还蛮大的，虽说这事算是过去了吧，但好多仙舟老板还没反应过来呢。
t_en: Can you not bring that up... If you ask me, I'd say the impact of the Arbor is quite significant. The crisis might be over, but many Xianzhou business owners haven't shaken it off just yet.

t_ch: 人家是长生种，十年八年只当是休假，可我们化外民就不一样了…零工倒是挺多的，可我想找一份稳定点的工作，这样也能在仙舟多待些时日啦！
t_en: As long-life species, they can afford to rest for years, but it's different for us Outworlders... There are many odd jobs available out there, but I'd prefer something more stable. That way, I can stay on Xianzhou for a longer time!

t_ch: 「靡靡赤龙、森森青松～六百余年凡尘中～如梦尽是空～」
t_en: "Dragons and demons, through thick and thin. Six hundred years, of dreaming within~"

t_ch: 哎！这位{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你走路怎么没声啊，吓春分一跳！
t_en: Hey, {F#miss}{M#sir}! How do you not make a sound while walking!? You scared me half to death!

t_ch: 你唱得真好听。
t_en: Your voice is beautiful.

t_ch: 真的吗？谢谢你！凌解在笔记本上留了很多曲子，春分好奇得很，所以自己尝试着练习着唱了唱。
t_en: Really? Thank you! Ling Jie wrote down stacks of songs in her notebook, and I've been so curious that I started practicing my singing.

t_ch: 也许春分唱得没有凌解好听，但唱曲这件事本身就很快乐，所以春分很满足！
t_en: I might not sing as well as Ling Jie, but singing makes me happy — so I've got no complaints at all!

t_ch: 对不起…
t_en: Sorry...

t_ch: 没关系啦！但是下次不要偷偷靠近春分哦！很吓人的。凌解留下的笔记本上说，她以前是名角儿，有很多坏东西对她居心叵测，千万要小心……
t_en: That's okay! But next time, don't sneak up on me like that — you freaked me out. Ling Jie's notebook says that she used to be famous, but lots of bad guys harbored ill intentions toward her. So please, be really careful out there...

t_ch: 啊！对不起，春分不是那个意思！春分是说{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}是好人，笔记本上也说了，能向小孩儿道歉的大人是好大人。
t_en: Oh, sorry — I didn't mean it like that! I meant to say, {F#miss}{M#sir} is one of the good guys. It says so in the notebook: Adults who apologize to kids must be good people.

t_ch: 凌解是谁？
t_en: Who's Ling Jie?

t_ch: 凌解是春分的前一世啊，据别人说她是个履历丰富又开朗的人，知道自己快蜕生结卵之前，专门给我准备了这本笔记，说是给我的礼物。
t_en: Ling Jie was my past life. People tell me that she was a cheery person with a rich and varied life. Before she molted and became an egg, she left this notebook for me as a present.

t_ch: 这本笔记上记录着各种各样的推荐，每一篇都写着「一生必须经历一次」、「超级推荐」…春分每一个都不想错过。
t_en: There's a crop of advice inside this notebook, each one described as "a once-in-a-lifetime experience" or "super recommended"... and I don't wanna miss out on any one of them.

t_ch: 你拿着别人的笔记本？
t_en: You took someone else's notebook?

t_ch: 凌解是春分的前一世，所以她也不算是春分的外人…再说了这本笔记就是她专门准备给春分的，是给我的礼物。
t_en: Ling Jie was my past life, so she's not really "someone else"... Besides, this notebook was especially gifted to me as a present.

t_ch: 这本笔记上记录着各种各样的推荐，每一篇都写着「一生必须经历一次」、「超级推荐」…春分每一个都不想错过。
t_en: There's a crop of advice inside this notebook, each one described as "a once-in-a-lifetime experience" or "super recommended"... and I don't wanna miss out on any one of them.

t_ch: 笔记上说，她希望春分也能像她那样，度过美好的一生。所以春分也把这本笔记当做旅行指南，想要好好体会一下「美好的一生」！
t_en: In the notebook, she hopes that I can live a good life, just like her. So I treat this notebook like a kind of travel guide as I try to experience "a good life"!

t_ch: 不说这个了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你找春分有什么事吗？
t_en: Enough of that, though. {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, what can I help you with?

t_ch: 就是随便聊聊天。
t_en: It's just a casual chat.

t_ch: 好呀好呀~春分最喜欢和人聊天了！
t_en: Okay okay~ my favorite thing to do is casual chats with people!

t_ch: 现在是做什么呢？
t_en: What are you doing now?

t_ch: 旅行呀！凌解的《前世笔记》上「一生必须经历一次」的事情还有很多没能经历…仔细想想，几百年的时间真的够用吗？春分有点担心！
t_en: Traveling! Ling Jie's reincarnation journal still has so many once-in-a-lifetime trips to experience... and thinking things through, will one lifetime be enough to see them all? I'm worried it won't be!

t_ch: …仔细想想，这些事情凌解真的都经历过吗？春分也不是很明白。
t_en: ...Hmmm, now that I think about it, did Ling Jie really experience all of these things? I'm not really too sure.

t_ch: 算了，春分不打算考虑得这么深入。
t_en: Forget about it, I shouldn't think so much about this kind of stuff.

t_ch: 你在唱什么呀？
t_en: What are you singing?

t_ch: 春分刚才唱的歌吗？这是持明时调《龙牙传》的唱词～故事的主角是一位「龙尊」，虽然春分还不太理解唱词，但曲调有种很悲伤的感觉。
t_en: The song I was just singing? It's a Vidyadhara elegy called Dragontooth Tales~ The story is about a high elder, and even though I don't quite understand what the lyrics mean, I know it's quite a mournful song.

t_ch: 「龙尊」是？
t_en: And high elders are...?

t_ch: 不要问小孩子这么难的问题啦！
t_en: Don't ask a kid such a difficult question!

t_ch: 龙尊就是…就是…就是龙尊！你还是去找个大人问问吧。
t_en: High elders are... well... they're high, and they're elders! It's probably better to go and ask a grown-up.

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 持明时调是什么？
t_en: What are Vidyadhara elegies?

t_ch: 唔…你看看这里。
t_en: Oh... check this out.

t_ch: 「持明时调是持明族的传统戏曲，起源于持明民歌，风格普遍忧伤婉约。」
t_en: "Vidyadhara elegies are the traditional music of the Vidyadhara people. Originating from their folk songs, the style is generally that of mournful dirges."

t_ch: 这是凌解记在笔记上的内容，看得出来她真的很喜欢持明时调。
t_en: That's what Ling Jie wrote down for me in her notebook. I can tell that she really loved these Vidyadhara elegies.

t_ch: 你说凌解是你的前世？
t_en: This Ling Jie — was that you in a past life?

t_ch: 咦？凌解说化外民都不太了解持明族的蜕生，就让春分来教教你吧！
t_en: Oh! Ling Jie wrote that Outworlders don't really understand the Vidyadhara's hatching rebirth, so let me explain everything to you!

t_ch: 持明一族寿数有限，每过几百年，就会重新化为龙卵，再从卵中重生。不过，重生之后就不会再记得上一世的记忆了。
t_en: The Vidyadhara has a fixed lifespan, and every few hundred years we revert to an egg so that we can be reborn. However, after the rebirth, none of our memories from the previous life is retained.

t_ch: 所以凌解和春分是两个人，凌解就是凌解，春分就是春分！
t_en: So Ling Jie and I are actually two people. Ling Jie is Ling Jie, and I am me!

t_ch: 没别的事了，你好好玩吧。
t_en: That's all from me. You have fun!

t_ch: 凌解在这本笔记上写了很多生活小妙招，比如被吓了一跳之后，如何假装镇定，厚着脸皮说自己早就预料到了…很可惜，我没有能实践成功。
t_en: Ling Jie wrote a lot of life tips out for me in her notebook, like how to play it cool when freaked out and pretend that you'd been expecting that... Unfortunately, I've never been able to pull it off successfully.

t_ch: 靡靡赤龙、森森青松～
t_en: When the dragon turns red, then the green pines shall shed.

t_ch: 六百余年凡尘中～如梦尽是空～
t_en: Pass like a dream out of sight and mind, six hundred years in this mortal bind~

t_ch: 我没事了，你好好玩吧。
t_en: I'm good. You have fun!

t_ch: 嗯嗯！等春分把持明时调练得更好听了，再给{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}唱一曲吧！
t_en: Yeah! Wait until I've practiced my Vidyadhara elegies some more, and I'll sing it even better for {F#miss}{M#sir} next time!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！之前那段唱词，春分最近又练了很久，你听听看有没有进步！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Sir}! I've been practicing that elegy you heard last time. Does it sound better now?

t_ch: 唔…咳咳…「靡靡赤龙、森森青松～六百余年凡尘中～如梦尽是空～」
t_en: Lalala~ ahem... "Dragons and demons, through thick and thin. Six hundred years, of dreaming within~"

t_ch: 如何？春分有没有进步！
t_en: Well? What do you think?

t_ch: 有诶！更好听了！
t_en: Yeah, it sounds better!

t_ch: 谢谢你！{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！春分也觉得自己进步了~
t_en: Thank you {F#miss}{M#sir}! I think it sounds better, too~

t_ch: 一般般，没什么区别。
t_en: Hmm, nah. Sounds the same to me.

t_ch: 唔…{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}真是不留情面！明明就是有进步。
t_en: Oh... {F#Miss}{M#Sir} is simply brutal! I've made big strides.

t_ch: …真不好意思，光顾着自说自话了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}找我有什么事？
t_en: ...sorry, I'm just talking to myself. {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, what can I help you with?

t_ch: 进出港报告管理办法确认，安全检查指南也…确认，新一轮化外民生活满意程度的调查问卷确认。文件备齐，再确认一下会议预约时间。
t_en: Management Strategy for Reporting Starskiff Traffic, check. Safety Inspection Guide, check. The life satisfaction survey for the new Outworlder batch, check. Documents are in order... now to confirm the schedule for the meeting.

t_ch: 嗯，时间也来得及，丹鼎司的出口药品分级相关的资料…需要找到对应的负责人确认。另外…商会的伴手礼订单，应该到货了，待会儿得派人去取。
t_en: Mm, there's still time. The relevant documentation for the Alchemy Commission's medicinal exports... requires confirmation from the person in charge. Also... souvenir bag orders for the merchant guilds should have arrived. I'll send someone to pick them up later.

t_ch: 嗯？怎么了，{F#小妹妹}{M#小弟弟}，我看你盯着姐姐这里半天了，你有什么需要我帮助你的吗？
t_en: Hmm? What is it, {F#young lady}{M#young man}? You've been staring at me for a while. How may I help you?

t_ch: 你在忙什么？
t_en: What are you busy with?

t_ch: 我是驭空大人的秘书，有很多文书要处理… 具体的内容嘛，我就不讲了。
t_en: I am Madam Yukong's secretary, charged with handling much documentation... I won't go into detail about my job scope.

t_ch: 你也不是真的对工作安排、会议文件很感兴趣，对吧？
t_en: You're not really interested in arranging work and poring over corporate files, are you?

t_ch: 只是觉得你很好看。
t_en: I just thought you looked nice, is all.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，那我就不客气地收下你的赞美了。
t_en: Hahaha, I'll accept your compliment then.

t_ch: 你知道我是驭空大人的秘书吧，负责好看也是我在天舶司的工作内容之一。
t_en: You know I am Madam Yukong's secretary, yes? Looking good is also part of my job in the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 哈哈，你当真了吗？只是开个玩笑而已。
t_en: Haha, are you serious? I was just kidding.

t_ch: 我可不是什么{F#小妹妹}{M#小弟弟}。
t_en: I'm not a {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，真可爱呀。
t_en: Hahaha, how cute.

t_ch: 那么，这是第一条来自天舶司主司秘书的建议——在仙舟上千万不可以以貌取人。跟你打招呼的人可能有几百岁了，这是很正常的情况。
t_en: Well then, here's the first tip from the Chief Secretary of the Head of the Sky-Faring Commission: Don't ever judge a book by its cover on Xianzhou. Anyone you talk to could be hundreds of years old. That is the norm here.

t_ch: 另外，这是第二条建议——姐姐说自己是姐姐的时候，你就只管听着就是了。
t_en: Also, second tip: When someone older than you speaks, it's best to just listen.

t_ch: 等等，我对你有些印象。「仙舟的重要客人」、「景元将军钦点的大善人」…从夕葵我这么一个小秘书的角度看起来，你可是位重要的大人物呢。
t_en: Hold on, I remember you. "An important guest of Xianzhou," "General Jing Yuan's appointed savior"... From the perspective of an insignificant secretary, you're a major character.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿，我有这么重要吗？
t_en: Oooh, am I really that important?

t_ch: 比你自己想象的更重要，我向你保证。
t_en: More than you think, I can assure you.

t_ch: 「景元将军钦点的大善人」？
t_en: "General Jing Yuan's appointed savior"?

t_ch: 怎么，不相信吗？
t_en: Oh, you don't believe me?

t_ch: 「大善人」这句话确实是我随口编的。不过我敢说，景元将军肯定也是这么想的。
t_en: I just made that up, this "savior" honorific. Though I'm sure that General Jing Yuan thinks so as well.

t_ch: 你这么捧我，有什么目的？
t_en: What are you up to, lavishing me with such praise?

t_ch: 嗯？你问我什么目的，当然是让你喜欢上我呀。姐姐这么可爱，你喜欢我也不会吃亏嘛。有这么意外吗？
t_en: Hmm? What am I up to? Just to make you like me, of course. I'm so cute, and you've got nothing to lose by returning the favor. Is that so surprising?

t_ch: 最喜欢姐姐了。
t_en: You're a sight for sore eyes.

t_ch: 啊，这么快就喜欢了吗，真不愧是姐姐我啊。
t_en: Oh, falling in love so quickly? I really must hand it to myself then.

t_ch: 我才不会上当。
t_en: I'm not falling for this.

t_ch: 上当，上什么当？姐姐不知道。
t_en: Falling? I don't know what you're talking about.

t_ch: 好了好了，姐姐还有正事要做，你看我手边这堆工作，我今天还准备准时下班呢。逗逗你还挺开心的，我把这个小礼物送给你吧。
t_en: All right, all right. I've got business to attend do. See that pile of work here? I still want to clock off work on time. It was fun teasing you, so here's a small gift for you.

t_ch: 要是你没什么别的事，我就接着忙了哟。
t_en: If there's nothing else, I'm getting back to work.

t_ch: 想多和姐姐再聊聊…
t_en: I want to talk to you some more...

t_ch: 啊，我明白了，想再多了解我一些对吧？好啊，那你问吧。
t_en: Ah, I get it. You want to know more about me? Fine, ask away.

t_ch: 你从小就这么好看吗？
t_en: Have you always been this pretty?

t_ch: 你的嘴真甜。不知道{F#小妹妹}{M#小弟弟}这张嘴，已经骗过多少人啦？
t_en: Oh, that sweet tongue. I wonder how many have been struck down by your glibness, {F#young lady}{M#young man}?

t_ch: 不过嘛，我对自己的姿色还是有点自知的，所以我也相信你是真诚的。谢谢你，你也非常可爱，是姐姐喜欢的类型。
t_en: Then again, I've always been confident in my looks, which is why I believe your words are sincere. Thank you, and you are very cute too, totally my style.

t_ch: 姐姐娶我。
t_en: Marry me, please.

t_ch: 哦，你才认识我多久就在想这种事了，嗯？
t_en: Oh, already at this stage when you've only known me for minutes, hmm?

t_ch: 让姐姐考虑一下……
t_en: Let me consider it...

t_ch: 现在还不行。
t_en: Not right now.

t_ch: 姐姐骂我。
t_en: Step on me, please.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，开玩笑还是要分场合的。
t_en: Hahaha, you must read the room before cracking such jokes.

t_ch: 这里是天舶司，要是再口不择言，你的蠢样整个银河都会知道了。
t_en: This is the Sky-Faring Commission. Keep this candor up, and the whole cosmos will soon know what your foolish face looks like.

t_ch: 姐姐我也喜欢你呀。
t_en: I like you too.

t_ch: 哦，这不是两情相悦了吗？
t_en: Oh, doesn't that mean we are lovers?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，谢谢你喜欢我。
t_en: Hahaha, thank you for liking me.

t_ch: 关于工作的事 …
t_en: About work...

t_ch: 工作的具体内容就不要问了，我还不想给驭空大人以外的人汇报工作呢。
t_en: Stop asking about what I do in detail. I'm not going to report my work to someone other than Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 但是我可以告诉你一个我的工作准则：准时下班。该做的工作都高效率地完成，然后到了下班时间后，我的努力就到此为止。
t_en: But I'll tell you a principle I stick to at work: Leaving work on time. Work with maximum efficiency while at work, but the moment I punch out, I turn work mode off.

t_ch: 你看这天舶司里，有很多喜欢用「加班来表明自己很努力」的人，到了下班时间偏不离开，宁愿磨磨蹭蹭到很晚才走，还以为自己很辛苦。
t_en: Just look around the Sky-Faring Commission — many love working overtime to prove their efforts. They refuse to get off on time, preferring to dawdle away till late at night. They even think themselves hardworking.

t_ch: 我就不一样了，唰唰唰地把工作完成，到了点我就走。「准点下班」和「早点睡觉」才是女孩最好的化妆品。
t_en: Me? I'm different. I'll finish up my tasks in a jiffy, and I'm gone when it's time to leave. "Leaving on time" and "sleeping early" are a girl's best friends to looking good.

t_ch: 关于驭空大人…
t_en: About Madam Yukong...

t_ch: 驭空大人呀，是我遇到过最好的上司。你们只见过她工作时候的样子吧，又严肃又认真，但实际上她私下的时候非常可爱。
t_en: Madam Yukong? She's the best boss I've ever met. You've only seen what she's like when she's working: Strict and serious. She's actually quite adorable in private.

t_ch: 我还会收到驭空大人送给我的衣服，不得不说，驭空大人的品味真的很不错，我经常穿着她给我的衣服出去逛街。
t_en: I've even received dresses gifted by Madam Yukong. I have to say, her taste is impeccable. I frequently wear those dresses of hers when I go out.

t_ch: 另外，我不是长得还挺可爱的嘛，经常有些不懂得分寸的家伙来惹事。这种时候驭空大人就太可靠了。
t_en: Also, my looks regularly invite trouble from persistent men who don't know their place. It's times like these when Madam Yukong is so dependable.

t_ch: 「再胆敢骚扰夕葵，我不但要你滚出天舶司，还要你滚出罗浮。」
t_en: "If you ever harass Xikui again, I won't just eject you out of the Sky-Faring Commission, and I'll also flush you off Luofu."

t_ch: 说实在话，我当时看着驭空大人，心里都已经小鹿乱撞了。
t_en: Honestly, when I first cast my eyes on Madam Yukong, my heart skipped a beat.

t_ch: 没别的事了，下次再见。
t_en: I don't have anything else to ask. See you next time.

t_ch: 嗯，那我们就下次再见吧。
t_en: All right, till next time then.

t_ch: 没别的事了，下次再见。
t_en: I don't have anything else to ask. See you next time.

t_ch: 嗯，那我们就下次再见吧。
t_en: All right, till next time then.

t_ch: 我看看，一份新的申请报告，由天舶司测速员发起的一档宣传交通法规的视频节目。有点意思……
t_en: Look, a new request report — it's a program about traffic regulations by the Sky-Faring Commission's speed testers. Interesting...

t_ch: 嗯？怎么了，{F#小妹妹}{M#小弟弟}，今天找我来有什么事吗？
t_en: Hmm? What is it, {F#young lady}{M#young man}? What can I do for you today?

t_ch: 关于停云…
t_en: About Tingyun...

t_ch: 很多人都在找她，我当然也想去找她，但驭空大人不让我去。
t_en: Many are looking for her. As much as I want to find her, Madam Yukong doesn't allow me to do so.

t_ch: 我猜想，驭空大人也知道这希望相当渺茫，因而才不希望我和她一起深陷其中吧。
t_en: I guess it's because Madam Yukong doesn't want me to get as deeply involved in the matter as her, knowing how slim the chance of finding Tingyun is.

t_ch: 既然如此，我就只能用自己的方式，陪在驭空大人身边，和她一起度过这段难捱的时光了。
t_en: Since that's the case, I can only stay by Madam Yukong's side and help her get through the trying times in my own way.

t_ch: 唉…这真的很难过，也很无力。每当这种时候，就不由得感受到人的渺小无助……
t_en: Ahhh... I feel so bad and powerless. I can't help but feel small and insignificant when such emotions hit me...

t_ch: 前段时间整理房间时，我从书柜里翻出了一本《客商一览醒迷》。是停云借给我的，竟然一直忘了还给她。
t_en: A while ago, while tidying up my room, I found a book titled A Guide to Merchants and Mysteries in my bookshelf. Tingyun lent it to me, and I keep forgetting to return it to her all this time.

t_ch: 真希望有一天，能有机会亲手把这本书还给她……
t_en: I really hope that I would be able to personally return this book to her one day...

t_ch: 小朋友，你在干什么呢？这是你该动的东西吗？
t_en: Little one, what are you doing here? Is this something you're allowed to touch?

t_ch: 对不起…
t_en: Sorry...

t_ch: 知道自己做得不对就赶紧放下吧。哎，现在的孩子真是没规没矩的。
t_en: You'd better put that down now if you know it's wrong. *sigh* Kids these days... unruly.

t_ch: 记住这是天舶司的第一条规矩，别乱动驭空大人的东西。
t_en: Remember the first rule of the Sky-Faring Commission: Don't touch Madam Yukong's things without permission.

t_ch: 你哪位？
t_en: Who are you again?

t_ch: 这孩子！什么叫我哪位？我哪位你也不能乱动驭空大人的东西啊！
t_en: How dare you ask who I am? That doesn't matter — you're not allowed to touch Madam Yukong's things!

t_ch: 趁着没人看到，赶紧放下吧。
t_en: Good thing no one saw. Hurry up and put it down.

t_ch: 我又不拿走…
t_en: I didn't take anything though...

t_ch: 你这叫什么话？不拿走也不能乱碰啊，更何况那些都是驭空大人的东西。
t_en: Talking back? Even if you didn't take anything, touching isn't allowed. Moreover, these are Madam Yukong's things.

t_ch: 趁着没人看到，赶紧放下吧。哎，现在的孩子真是没规没矩的。
t_en: Good thing no one saw. Hurry up and put it down. *sigh* Kids these days... unruly.

t_ch: 我叫岩明，是「天驱商会」驻天舶司的公使，算得上半个市舶官。
t_en: I am Yanming, a Celestial Guild envoy attached to the Sky-Faring Commission. You can consider me half a Maritime Trade Officer.

t_ch: 过去我随着商团在天外出使了约有一百五十年，你如果有任何不懂的，可以直接问我，而不是自己到处乱碰。
t_en: Previously, I followed the merchant guild and was dispatched to other star systems for about <unbreak>150</unbreak> years. If there's something you want to know, feel free to ask me, and don't touch stuff without permission.

t_ch: 「商会」是什么？
t_en: What is a "merchant guild"?

t_ch: 我不知道你听说过多少商会，「鸣火」商会听说过没有？
t_en: I don't know how many merchant guilds you've heard of. Does the Whistling Flames guild ring any bells?

t_ch: 停云所在的天外行商团？
t_en: The intergalactic merchant guild that Tingyun is in?

t_ch: 没错，天舶司担负着与异族互通有无的重任，而商会则是这其中最重要的一环。
t_en: That's right. The Sky-Faring Commission bears the salient duty of exchanging mutual necessities with other races, and the merchant guilds are a key part of this process.

t_ch: 那个小妮子担任贸易使节后，先后连接起了十六个世界的贸易使团，还把海市办成了星海盛典，「鸣火」居功至伟。
t_en: After that girl took up the mantle of trade envoy, she linked up the trade delegations of sixteen worlds in succession. She even turned the Grand Fair into an intergalactic festival; the Whistling Flames guild played an integral part in all this.

t_ch: …我敢说那姑娘将来肯定能接下驭空大人的职务。
t_en: ...I dare say that girl is the next in line to succeed Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 没有听说过。
t_en: I've never heard of this.

t_ch: 看来是我自作多情了。不过你既然是化外民，想要与仙舟互通贸易，还是应该了解一下仙舟的天外行商团。
t_en: I was hoping for too much then. But since you're an Outworlder, if you wish to engage in mutual trade with Xianzhou, you should at least understand the workings of Xianzhou's intergalactic merchant guilds.

t_ch: 停云小妮子所在的「鸣火」商会先后连接起了十六个世界的贸易使团，还把海市办成了星海盛典。
t_en: The Whistling Flames guild that Tingyun works for got in bed with the trade delegations from sixteen worlds and turned the Grand Fair into an intergalactic festival.

t_ch: …我敢说那姑娘将来肯定能接下驭空大人的职务。
t_en: ...I dare say that girl is the next in line to succeed Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 你是在倚老卖老？
t_en: Are you trying to lord your seniority over me?

t_ch: 过去我在天外行商的时候也遇到过你这样的家伙，说话耿直，不看气氛。
t_en: I've met people like you during my time in the guilds beyond the sky — straight to the point, and can't read the room.

t_ch: 不过，我不讨厌你这样的人。在生意场上，你这样的人比那些口腹蜜剑、城府颇深的人要好得多。
t_en: But I don't mind people like you in business. Your kind is still better than the two-faced backstabbers.

t_ch: 你没听说过「天驱商会」，总听说过「鸣火」吧？
t_en: If you never heard of the Celestial Guild, you've at least heard of the Whistling Flames, right?

t_ch: 停云小妮子所在的「鸣火」商会先后连接起了十六个世界的贸易使团，还把海市办成了星海盛典。
t_en: The Whistling Flames guild that Tingyun works for got in bed with the trade delegations from sixteen worlds and turned the Grand Fair into an intergalactic festival.

t_ch: …我敢说那姑娘将来肯定能接下驭空大人的职务。
t_en: ...I dare say that girl is the next in line to succeed Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 嗐！我和你说这些干什么，你肯定没什么兴趣吧。别见怪，不管是你们短生种还是我们长生种，年纪大了都有点好为人师之嫌。
t_en: I'm boring you with all this rambling. However long-lived your species, when you get older, you tend to lecture on like a teacher.

t_ch: 刚才我的态度也有点儿强硬，我把这个送给你赔罪，希望你不要计较。
t_en: My attitude was a little rough, so I want to make it up by giving you this.

t_ch: 不过我再强调一下，你如果有任何不懂的，可以直接问我，而不是自己到处乱碰。
t_en: But let me emphasize that if there's anything you want to know, just ask me instead of touching things.

t_ch: 有一些不懂的想问问。
t_en: I'd like to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 你问吧，我会考虑之后回答你，有些不便回答的，希望你也能理解。
t_en: Go ahead. I'll mull it over and tell you what I can.

t_ch: 你多大年纪了？
t_en: How old are you?

t_ch: 呃，我只能告诉你我已经足够老了，具体的数字不重要。
t_en: Uh, I'll tell you I'm old enough. The specific age isn't important.

t_ch: 这个数字是几百还是几千，反正对你而言，都同样老得不可思议。
t_en: Whether it's in the hundreds or thousands, it will still sound ancient to you.

t_ch: 这样吧，我给你一条忠告，就算再好奇，在仙舟上别问女孩子这样的问题…在任何世界都最好别问。
t_en: Let me give you some advice: Never ask a girl on Xianzhou that question... Or in any world, for that matter.

t_ch: 你在这里做什么工作？
t_en: What do you do here?

t_ch: 简单来说，我主要负责向商会通知天舶司的命令、向天舶司请示商会的要求。
t_en: Simply put, I'm in charge of relaying orders from the Sky-Faring Commission to the guilds, and passing guild requests back.

t_ch: 「天驱商会」并不是纯粹的官方商会，有很多个体小商户参会。正因如此，我这个驻天舶司公使就可以帮助两边沟通。
t_en: The Celestial Guild is not one big body but a consortium of many smaller guilds, so I help both sides communicate with each other.

t_ch: 不过，我倒也不是一直在干这个工作。再往前几百年，我是一艘商船的队长；再往前几百年，我是黉学里的商学教授……算啦，都只是工作罢了。
t_en: However, several centuries back, I was a captain of a trade ship. Centuries before that, I was a business professor in a lyceum... Never mind, they're just jobs.

t_ch: 工作经验越久，朴素的道理就越实用——认真对待工作，工作也会认真对待你。
t_en: The more experienced you are, the more you'll realize that how serious you treat a job is how serious it treats you.

t_ch: 关于商会…
t_en: About the guild...

t_ch: 自从诸般灾难尘埃落定，「罗浮」停泊下来之后，「商会」就一直是仙舟的经济命脉。仙舟上大大小小的商会，有些有官方背景，有些则纯粹是民间组织。
t_en: Ever since the calamities ended and the Luofu settled down, guilds have been Xianzhou's lifeblood. Some of them are government-backed, and some are purely civilian.

t_ch: 你知道停云吧，她就是天外行商团「鸣火」的首席代表。那个小妮子圆转如意，擅长与人沟通，天生是个做生意的料。
t_en: Tingyun is the Head Representative of the intergalactic merchant guild, Whistling Flames. She's witty and a great communicator. She was born to do business.

t_ch: 你听说过仙舟人的贸易节庆「海市」吗？在停云小妮子的支持督办下，也变成了声名远扬星海的盛典。
t_en: Have you heard of the Grand Fair in Xianzhou? Tingyun turned that into an intergalactic festival.

t_ch: 「灯笼火树，争然九陌之时，舞席歌筵，大启千灯之夜」，办得真好啊……
t_en: "Sea of fiery lights illuminate the countless suburbs, while feasts and songs unveil nights of innumerable glows"... Nicely done.

t_ch: 不好意思，话题有些跑偏了。不过，你也大概理解商会有多重要了吧。
t_en: Pardon me for getting off-tangent, but you probably realize how vital the guilds are now...

t_ch: 关于驭空大人…
t_en: About Madam Yukong...

t_ch: 驭空大人啊…算是我的直属上司。她的能力自不必说，但真正让人佩服的还是她的性格。办事强硬、待人温和，关键时刻雷厉风行……
t_en: You could say Madam Yukong is my boss. Her competence is undeniable, but her flexible adaptability to different situations is even more impressive.

t_ch: 虽然她自称在商业上缺乏天赋，但我看来决然不是。驭空大人常常不动言语就能镇住那些投机倒把、心怀鬼胎的说客，很少有人在她的面前含糊其辞。
t_en: Although she claimed to be lacking in trade acumen, I beg to differ, as she could often shut schemers and opportunists down without a word, and few can manage to play with words before her.

t_ch: 在我看来，虽然和八面玲珑的做法有些差异，但终究是殊途同归。驭空大人在场时，商业洽谈也顺利了不少。
t_en: To me, the end is the same even when the approach is not. The negotiations go a lot smoother with Madam Yukong present.

t_ch: 没别的事了，我先走啦。
t_en: That's all. I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: 好啊，恕不远送。
t_en: Sure, have a nice day.

t_ch: 没别的事了，我先走啦。
t_en: That's all. I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: 好啊，恕不远送。
t_en: Sure, have a nice day.

t_ch: 「顺颂商祺…」
t_en: Best regards...

t_ch: 和仙舟人做生意，这些化外民的小心思真是越来越多了。
t_en: The Outworlders are getting shrewder when trading with Xianzhou.

t_ch: 嗯，是你呀。怎么了，有什么不懂的想要问我吗？
t_en: It's you. Is there anything you'd like to ask me?

t_ch: 关于停云…
t_en: About Tingyun...

t_ch: 有不少同僚都相信她还活着，发誓一定要找到她。但我嘛…我可不是那么不现实的人。
t_en: Many of my colleagues believe she is still alive and pledge to find her. As for me... I'm more realistic.

t_ch: 但我也在找她，和大家一起找她…原因很简单，因为驭空大人觉得她还活着，想要找到她。所以我会和驭空大人一起找她。仅此而已。
t_en: Having said that, I'm still looking for her alongside everyone else, simply because Madam Yukong thinks she is alive and wants to find her. I'll follow Madam Yukong to find her - it's as simple as that.

t_ch: 即使我千不信万不信，也不能任由驭空大人陷在这件事当中。
t_en: Even though I don't believe it, I won't have Madam Yukong getting too deeply involved in the matter alone.

t_ch: 驭空大人将「鸣火」交托于我，让我替停云好生操持。我看着那些她的旧部下，实在不知道要怎么向他们解释现状……
t_en: Madam Yukong entrusted me with the Whistling Flames to take care of for Tingyun. Looking at her former subordinates, I honestly didn't know how to explain the situation to them...

t_ch: 哎…这世间尽是些，让人笑不出来的玩笑。
t_en: *sigh* ...The world is full of jokes I can't bring myself to laugh at.

t_ch: 可惜了，可惜。我还以为你够资格，是我误会了。这一方小小竞锋舰，自称吸引了天下的习武高手，本以为能让我兴奋一下，看来也不过如此。哎……
t_en: Oh, what a shame! I thought you worthy, but I was wrong. This little Skysplitter boasted about attracting the greatest martial artists in the cosmos and got me all hyped up, but what a letdown it has been.

t_ch: 能不能把话说完，什么资格？
t_en: What do you mean by "worthy"?

t_ch: 我在寻找强者。我原以为你是，但很可惜，那只是我的错觉，你的纯度还不够。
t_en: I'm looking for a formidable opponent. I thought you were the one, but alas, that was just wishful thinking on my part. You're not strong enough.

t_ch: 我倒是看你才是不够资格的！
t_en: You're the one who's not worthy!

t_ch: 从来只有我筛选挑战者的资格，你口出狂言，但实力不济…你的纯度还不够。
t_en: I'm the one who decides who's worthy and who's not. You're bold to make such a claim, but you're simply not strong enough.

t_ch: 别误会，你在这群庸才之中，也算是实力顶尖了。这也是我连叹「可惜」的原因。
t_en: Don't get me wrong. You're already a standout among these clowns, but that's exactly why I found it a shame.

t_ch: 你看看他们。出拳无力，剑法不精，脚步松散，反应迟钝…如此水平也敢来丢人现眼。
t_en: Just look at them. Feeble punches, unrefined swordplay, clumsy footwork, sluggish reflexes... It's a mystery why they even think they're good enough to be here.

t_ch: 这群庸才就差把「友谊万岁」写在脸上了。全部加起来，都赶不上我一根脚指头。
t_en: They might as well fly a flag that says, "I'm just happy to be here!" All of them combined aren't even close to holding a candle to me!

t_ch: 要这么说的话，你很强咯？
t_en: You must be very strong then?

t_ch: 强者不会向弱者宣扬自己的强大。强者只需要矗立在那里，便说明了一切。
t_en: The strong do not parade their ability before the weak. Their mere presence speaks volumes.

t_ch: 嘴上说说而已，报名了吗？
t_en: Talk is cheap. Did you even sign up for the competition?

t_ch: 报名？跟这些小孩子玩过家家的游戏？不要侮辱我了，我不会报名的。
t_en: And play house with these kids? The thought alone is insulting enough. I refuse to lower myself to that.

t_ch: 我击败过武装考古学派的特遣部队，手撕兽舰，脸接离子炮…我要是登上擂台，在场的人通通活不过我简单一招，那这比赛还有什么看头？比赛早就已经结束了。
t_en: I've defeated the Armed Archaeologists' task forces, taken down beast ships, and deflected ion cannons point-blank. One move is all I need to beat everyone here. If I step into that arena, the match would be over before it even begins, and where would be the fun in that?

t_ch: 再者说来，我和仙舟签订过互不侵犯协议，不会在联盟境内动武。要是一不小心忘了收力，将罗浮击沉了，你说谁能负责？
t_en: Besides, I've signed a non-aggression pact with the Xianzhou and pledged to abstain from violence within the alliance's borders. If I fail to keep my strength in check and sink the Luofu, who's to be held accountable for that?

t_ch: 你也太狂了，来打一架吧。
t_en: Confident, huh. Up for a spar?

t_ch: 年轻人常常不知好歹，我不会对你出手的，这是为了你好。
t_en: Fledglings don't appreciate kindness anymore these days. For your own good, I'm not going to spar with you.

t_ch: 没想到，你这人还挺温柔？
t_en: Didn't peg you for the kindly type.

t_ch: 呵呵，我经常听到有人对我说「范勇你真的很温柔啊。」
t_en: Heh, I hear things like "Oh, Fanyong, you're too nice" all the time.

t_ch: 唉，无敌对一位武人来说，是多么寂寞和悲哀。我还得继续寻找那「尚能一战」的对手，你要是想到了什么，可以随时告诉我。
t_en: Ah, it's so lonely at the top. But I won't give up on searching for a worthy opponent. If you ever think of somebody that fits the bill, let me know.

t_ch: 唉，纯度实在是太低了——你又回来啦？这比赛简直是无聊透顶。
t_en: Sigh, so weak... Oh, you're back? This competition is painfully boring.

t_ch: 想和你再聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to chat with you some more.

t_ch: 好提议，我就陪你聊聊。
t_en: Sure. I'll keep you company then.

t_ch: 关于比赛的选手…
t_en: About the contestants...

t_ch: 哼，也就现在是和平年代，放在丰饶民战争时期，他们玩弄的那套把戏，与其说是武，不如说是舞。
t_en: Hmph, they're lucky to be living in peaceful times. During the Denizens of Abundance Wars, those "moves" would have been more like dance arts than martial arts.

t_ch: 尤其是那些鼓吹着「奥义、秘传、秘技」的庸才，未免有些太过聒噪了。只有将自己视为弱者的人，才会去依赖技巧，真正的强者只需要纯粹的力量。
t_en: Especially those that prattle on and on about "ultimate moves," "secret arts," "arcane techniques" and whatnot. They weren't lying when they say it's the empty vessels that make the most noise. Only the weak rely on such tricks. True strength needs no embellishment, just raw power.

t_ch: 我给你推荐几名强者。
t_en: I can recommend some formidable opponents to you.

t_ch: 那他们最好是「强者」。
t_en: They'd better be as "formidable" as you say.

t_ch: 我去请飞霄将军。
t_en: I'll go get General Feixiao.

t_ch: 堂堂天击将军，和我在罗浮仙舟外私斗输掉，这样传出去影响不好，有碍于仙舟联盟团结稳定，尤其是对青丘军来说。
t_en: It'd be terrible if news of the great Merlin's Claw losing to me in a duel outside the Xianzhou Luofu spreads. It could shake the unity and stability of the Xianzhou Alliance, especially among the Verdant Knights.
